commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WSL preflight version parsing to read a semantic version from the line containing the WSL product token and adds setup-engine regression tests for localized, hyphenated, NUL-stripped, and adjacent-component version output.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only accepts `WSL\s+version:`, so hyphenated and localized labels fall through to the terminal parse failure path. I did not run a live Windows WSL runtime in this read-only Linux checkout.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the focused parser and regression-test fix; the remaining work is normal maintainer review and validation.

target.title: [Repo Assist] fix(setup): accept hyphenated and localized WSL version labels in preflight
target.body:
🤖 *This is an automated pull request from Repo Assist, with follow-up maintainer updates.*

## Summary

Fixes #661 — `preflight-wsl` fails on non-English Windows even when WSL is installed and at a supported version.

## Root Cause

`wsl.exe --version` returns localized, human-readable text and can also emit UTF-16LE/NUL-interleaved output on some Windows builds. The old parser normalized NUL/BOM characters, but still required the English label shape:

```text
WSL version: 2.7.3.0
```

That failed for valid localized output such as:

```text
WSL-Version: 2.7.7.0
Versión de WSL: 2.7.3.0
```

So setup could report `WSL version output did not include a parseable WSL version` even when WSL was installed, up to date, and `wsl.exe --version` exited successfully.

## Fix

The parser no longer tries to match translated label text like `version`, `Version`, `Versión`, etc.

Instead, `WslInstallSupport.TryParseWslVersion` now:

1. Normalizes NUL/BOM output.
2. Splits `wsl.exe --version` output by line.
3. Finds a line containing the stable `WSL` product token.
4. Parses the semantic version on that same line.
5. Avoids accidentally reading adjacent component versions such as `WSLg`, `Windows`, or kernel versions.

This keeps the fix narrow while removing the localization dependency from the version-label words.

## Locale Coverage

Added regression coverage using the upstream `microsoft/WSL` localization resources:

- Source checked: `localization/strings/*/Resources.resw`
- Resource key: `MessagePackageVersions`
- Coverage: all 22 currently published locale labels, including Czech, Danish, German, Spanish, Finnish, French, Hungarian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Dutch, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Swedish, Turkish, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese.

The parser is also covered for:

- English, German, Spanish, Japanese-style, and no-separator labels.
- NUL-stripped UTF-16-style output.
- Multi-line output with `Kernel`, `WSLg`, and `Windows` component versions.
- False-positive guard where only adjacent component version lines are present.

## Validation

Local validation completed on Windows:

```text
./build.ps1                                                     ✅ passed
dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests/...              ✅ 230/230
dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/... --no-restore      ✅ 2045 passed, 29 skipped integrations
dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/... --no-restore        ✅ 934/934
```

Structured review also reported no accepted/actionable findings.

## Notes / Trade-offs

Parsing `wsl.exe --version` remains less ideal than a locale-independent WSL API or capability probe. However, the current implementation now depends on the stable product token `WSL` plus a semantic version, not on translated human-readable label text.

Longer term, the cleanest design may be to make the version check non-blocking and let the actual `wsl --install --distribution ... --name ... --location ...` command validate capability directly, with cleanup and clearer error handling if WSL lacks those flags.
